title: manulea altissima subsp. longifolia
intro: Manulea altissima subsp. longifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae, commonly known as a type of Manulea, a genus of plants native to South Africa. Traditionally, Manulea altissima subsp. longifolia is used for its medicinal properties, which include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of respiratory infections, as well as acting as an antispasmodic to relieve muscle cramps and spasms. Manulea altissima subsp. longifolia is typically used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
